source.title: Accident Investigation Report - MC 809 Platform A (Ursa) - Slip/Trip/Fall Injury

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 809

actions_taken: Stop Work Authority utilized immediately following incident
Crew identified and wrapped protruding sharp metal pieces
JSA revised to include jagged edges and uneven surfaces as hazards
IP medically evacuated by helicopter at 1010 hours
IP received 11 staples and 4 stitches at Medical Center
IP placed on light duty status and returned to SafeZone office on 24 February 2025
event.summary: On 23 February 2025 at approximately 0830 hours at Mississippi Canyon 809 Platform A (Ursa), a SafeZone worker slipped backwards while securing felt and cleaning inside a Pressurized Welding Enclosure. The worker's right leg contacted a jagged edge of an I-beam, resulting in a 3-to-4-inch laceration. The worker fell 12-18 inches from an upper to lower level, insufficient to deploy fall protection equipment. The worker was medically evacuated and received 11 staples and 4 stitches. Investigation found inadequate hazard identification during pre-job assessment, lack of supervision, and failure to identify jagged edges and uneven surfaces in the JSA.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations should consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Work area should have a more thorough hazard assessment performed
All personnel should be made aware of hazards before starting work
Hazards identified at time of assessment should be mitigated to reduce or eliminate threat to personnel before starting work
key_phrases: lack of identifying the jagged edges

hazards.name: Jagged metal edges and uneven surfaces in confined work area

threats.name: Inadequate hazard identification during pre-job assessment
Worker loss of footing on uneven surface
Lack of situational awareness of foot placement

consequences.name: Laceration injury to worker requiring medical evacuation
Worker placed on light duty status

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify and document workplace hazards before work commences
controls.lod_basis: Primary planning control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: the initial JSA hazard identification for the work being performed did not mention jagged edges inside the work area or uneven surfaces creating hazards for personnel
The lack of supervision and these items having not been identified during the initial hazard identification allowed existing hazards to remain unaddressed
human error due to the inadequacy of the pre-job assessment

controls.name: Supervision during hazard assessment and work execution
controls.barrier_role: Ensure hazards are properly identified and mitigated
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory oversight to verify hazard controls are in place

supporting_text: Supervision – Inadequate supervision while identifying the hazards in the work area during hazard assessment and mitigation for the hazards
The lack of supervision and these items having not been identified during the initial hazard identification allowed existing hazards to remain unaddressed

controls.name: Fall protection PPE (self-retracting lifeline)
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fall injuries when working at heights
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment as primary defense against falls

supporting_text: The IP was working at heights, which required him to wear fall protection personal protective equipment (PPE), consisting of self-retracting lifeline (SRL) at the time of the incident

the uneven surface that the IP was working on had a 12-18 inch drop down to the next working level. When the IP slipped and lost his footing, he fell 12-18 inches from the upper level to the lower level. This slight difference in elevation was not a great enough drop to deploy the IP's fall protection

controls.name: Worker situational awareness and foot placement
controls.barrier_role: Maintain awareness of work environment and hazards
controls.lod_basis: Worker behavior as primary defense during task execution

supporting_text: Human Error – The lack of awareness by the IP concerning the uneven surfaces and foot placement during the job risk analysis and while performing the work
The IP took a step back from an elevated working surface, lost his footing and slipped backwards

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Halt work when unsafe conditions are identified
controls.lod_basis: Recovery action to prevent escalation after incident

supporting_text: Stop Work Authority (SWA) was utilized by the on-site personnel immediately following the incident
a safety standdown was conducted with the rest of the crew to address any safety concerns

controls.name: Platform medic and medical assessment
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical assessment and treatment
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: At 0845 hours, the incident was reported to the platform medic, who completed an assessment of the IP's injuries
A decision was made to send the IP to a medical facility for further evaluation onshore

controls.name: Medical evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to medical facilities
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response infrastructure to enable treatment

supporting_text: The IP then left MC 809 A on a Medevac helicopter at 1010 hours and landed at Bristow heliport in Houma, Louisiana at 1136 hours
The IP was then transported to the Medical Center at 1150 hours

controls.name: Post-incident hazard mitigation (wrapping sharp edges)
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate identified hazards to prevent recurrence
controls.lod_basis: Corrective action to prevent similar incidents

supporting_text: The crew was able to identify the potential exposures of sharp pieces of metal that were present and mitigate the hazard by wrapping any protruding pieces of metals that were found

controls.name: JSA revision and update process
controls.barrier_role: Update procedures to reflect identified hazards
controls.lod_basis: Corrective action to improve future hazard identification

supporting_text: The JSA was then revised and added the potential for jagged edges and uneven surfaces as hazards and determined how to safely mitigate those hazards

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
